fragility_points: Large overnight or morning crypto selloff before noon ET.
Binance-specific operational or chart-timing issue near settlement.
Single-minute settlement microstructure amplifying transient price moves.
key_assumptions: Binance ETH/USDT remains operational and the noon ET candle maps cleanly to contract language.
No major downside catalyst hits ETH before the Apr 17 noon ET resolution minute.
main_logical_chain: Contract asks about one specific Binance ETH/USDT minute close above 2200 at noon ET on Apr 17.
Current ETH/USDT context is materially above 2200, so the outside-view prior favors Yes.
Because crypto is volatile and the market settles on one minute rather than a broad average, near-certainty is too aggressive.
Therefore Yes remains favored, but at about 88% rather than the market's 94.5%.
main_thesis: Yes is still the right side because ETH/USDT is materially above 2200 close to resolution, but the market slightly overstates certainty for a one-minute Binance-close contract.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
source_quality_view: High confidence in contract mechanics from Polymarket rules, moderate confidence in contextual price level from independent price pages/search snippets, with medium evidence independence overall.
strongest_disconfirmers: Settlement depends on a single Binance one-minute close at noon ET, so a brief downside move is enough for No.
Recent context included at least one nearby daily close below 2200, showing sub-2200 prints are still plausible.
strongest_supports: ETH/USDT contextual spot was around the mid-2300s during research, leaving a roughly 6-7% cushion above 2200.
Less than a day remained to resolution, favoring the currently above-strike side absent a sharp selloff.
Recent daily ETH/USDT context was mostly above 2200 over the prior week.
timing_relevance: This is a date- and timezone-sensitive single-minute market resolving at 12:00 PM ET on 2026-04-17, so proximity to resolution and intraday volatility matter heavily.
unresolved_ambiguities: Direct live extraction from Binance was limited, leaving small residual ET-to-candle mapping uncertainty.
No fresh Apr 17 morning check yet; current view depends on Apr 16 spot context persisting.
what_would_change_view: A fresh check showing ETH/USDT near or below 2200 on Apr 17 morning, or Binance-specific operational problems near settlement, would move the estimate down materially.
